title: Below Specification
description: The clockwork rats from World 3 are deteriorating in the World 4 tunnels. They aren't malicious — they're stressed. The tunnels run hotter than Cogsworth Junction and their springs are winding too fast in an environment they weren't designed for. The ones that detonate aren't attacking. They're overloading.

offer.text: The junction is scored with tiny wheel-tracks, looping endlessly. A rat clatters past on its patrol — springs audibly overwound, ticking fast and wrong, like a heart that can't find its resting rate.

offer.speaker: Mage
offer.text: The springs are stressed. This heat — they weren't built for it. The detonations aren't aggression. They're overload.

offer.speaker: Cleric
offer.text: Is there somewhere they could run that isn't a combat encounter?

accept.speaker: Mage
accept.text: Cooler section, older air circulation — the Cogsworth-era construction breathes differently. If we can find it, they could survive there.

decline.text: The rats keep looping. The loops keep tightening. The tunnels keep the heat.

in_progress.speaker: Mage
in_progress.text: They can't be herded — they follow their patrol routes. But patrols orient on resonance. Give them a sound that reads as Brasston, and the routes will drift toward it on their own.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Sprocket
ready_to_turn_in.text: The chip held? The little ones found it?

complete.speaker: Sprocket
complete.text: Ha! My calibration chip is a lighthouse for clockwork rats two worlds from home. That's the best thing any part of mine has ever done, and I once fixed the Junction's grand chime blindfolded.

complete.speaker: Sprocket
complete.text: They moved toward what sounded familiar. Everything does. That's not a mechanism thing — that's just true, and mechanisms are honest about it.

complete.speaker: Sprocket
complete.text: Check on them when you pass through. Things that loop still notice who visits.

objectives.description: Understand what the detonating rats actually are.
Find the cooler Cogsworth-era tunnel section.
Ask Sprocket for a resonance calibration chip that sounds like home.
Place the beacon in the cool section.
Tell Sprocket what his chip became.

cleric.description: Dialogue-only (Safety Officer): 'I wasn't sure this counted as medicine. I think it might. I think keeping things from breaking down is the same as healing them.'

unlocks_dialogue.note: The settled cluster near the beacon becomes a recurring passive: occasionally drops a random rare component when the party passes through that tunnel section. Ongoing-drop wiring is overworld/battle's when convenient — documented here, not blocking.

cross_world_connection: The Tempo of the Mainspring's fragment (Chain B W3), if collected after this quest, includes one additional line: 'They moved toward something that felt like home. A sound they recognized. I think that's what most motion is — moving toward what sounds familiar.'
_wiring_notes: Giver is an interactable (rat patrol junction, TallyWall pattern) spawning post rat-encounter chapter flag. Step 1 emitter: examine interact at the junction (the offer dialogue IS the identification — emit on offer-accept). Step 2 emitter quest_world4_below_specification_cool_section_found: entering the Cogsworth-era ventilated section; if mapped_route (W3 lamplighter quest) is in inventory it's marked on the map directly — without it, findable by exploration (slower, not blocked). Step 3+5 are CROSS-WORLD talk targets — sprocket_brasston lives in Brasston (W3): this quest deliberately exercises fast-travel/backtracking (crystal warp W4→W3 is 150g per the tier pricing). Sprocket's chip line varies if world3_delay_in_everything is incomplete: he still helps, seven seconds late, and the ready_to_turn_in/complete lines gain the leading '...' delay markers. Step 4 emitter: beacon placement interact in the cool section. SLOW-BURN per doc: rat population shift happens over following chapters — presentation detail (decreasing hot-section spawns, growing beacon cluster), not quest state.
